petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification as an employment-based immigrant pursuant to section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien with extraordinary ability in the sciences. No further details about the petitioner's specific field, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision. Notably, the petitioner had already been granted adjustment of status to lawful permanent residency on April 22, 2005 on the basis of an approved second preference employment-based immigrant petition.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was dismissed as moot because the petitioner had already adjusted to lawful permanent resident status on April 22, 2005, based on an approved second preference (EB-2) employment-based immigrant petition. Because the alien had adjusted to lawful permanent resident status, further pursuit of the extraordinary ability matter was deemed moot. The AAO did not reach the merits of whether the petitioner had established sustained national or international acclaim.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: The appeal was dismissed on procedural/mootness grounds rather than on the merits, because the petitioner had already obtained lawful permanent resident status through a separate approved EB-2 petition while the EB-1A appeal was pending.
